failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $328,800 below minimum requirement $3,500,001
FICO Score score 715 below minimum requirement 720
FICO Score score 715 below minimum 720 required for Primary Residence, SFR
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

Cover Letter for Transcript of Tax Return Form
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet
Consent for Release of Information SSA-3288
Evidence of Delivery and Receipt of VOE

missing_core_variables.why_it_matters: DTI must be within program cap; cannot validate program eligibility without it.

income: RATIOS AND QUALIFYING – FULL AND ALT DOC

specifications: Must be current and unexpired government-issued photo identification (Driver's License, Passport, State ID, or Military ID)
Must show borrower's full legal name matching the loan application
Must include photo, date of birth, and expiration date
Must be legible and all four corners visible

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity must be verified for Veronica Marie Montes to validate all loan documents, credit reports, and authorizations
Required for compliance with PATRIOT Act and customer identification program requirements

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct borrower name, SSN, and current address
Must specify appropriate tax years for income verification (most recent 1-2 years)
Must be signed within 120 days of closing
Must authorize transcript retrieval for 1099 income documentation type

reasons_needed.reason: IRS Form 4506-C is required per NQMF Full Documentation guidelines for all borrowers
Required to authorize IRS tax transcript retrieval to validate 1099 income documentation for self-employment/independent contractor income

specifications: Must be fully executed with all signatures from buyer and seller
Must show purchase price of $411,000 consistent with loan scenario
Must identify subject property address at 18642 Vanderlip Avenue, Cerritos, CA 90703
Must include all addenda, amendments, and counter-offers
Must show earnest money deposit amount and holder
Must include closing date and contingency periods
Must identify all parties to the transaction

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires executed contract to confirm purchase price, parties, and terms
Required to verify LTV calculation of 80% based on $411,000 purchase price and $328,800 loan amount
Required to identify seller and confirm arm's length transaction

specifications: Must include signed borrower certification of intent to occupy as primary residence
Must specify intended occupancy date (within 60 days of closing for primary residence)
Must acknowledge penalties for occupancy fraud
Must be consistent with loan application declarations

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy must be certified to confirm eligibility for 80% LTV and Flex Supreme program requirements
Occupancy type drives documentation requirements, reserve requirements, and pricing

specifications: Must include 1099 forms for the most recent 12 months of income as required by the 1099 (12 month) program
Must show borrower name matching loan application
Must identify payer/source of 1099 income
Must show gross income amount received
Must be from the same income source to demonstrate continuity
Must include all 1099 forms received (1099-MISC, 1099-NEC, or applicable form types)
reasons_needed: 1099 income documentation is required for qualification under the Flex Supreme 1099 (12 month) program
Income source and continuity must be verified for the required period
Gross income amounts are needed to calculate qualifying income
Guidelines require 1099 income from the same source for income stability verification
document_requests.guideline_reference: STABILITY AND CONTINUANCE OF SELF-EMPLOYMENT - Borrowers receiving 1099 income (filing as Schedule C or E) are eligible provided that the 1099 income is from the same source

specifications: Must include most recent 1 year federal personal tax return (Form 1040)
Must be complete with all pages and schedules
Must be signed and dated
Must include Schedule C (if sole proprietor) or Schedule E (if applicable for 1099 income reporting)
Must show borrower name and SSN matching loan application
Must reflect 1099 income consistent with 1099 forms provided
reasons_needed: Tax returns are required to verify and calculate qualifying income for 1099 borrowers
Schedule C or E is needed to analyze self-employment/1099 income per NQMF guidelines
Tax return income must reconcile with 1099 forms to support income continuity and stability
Required documentation for income qualification under Flex Supreme program
document_requests.guideline_reference: SELF-EMPLOYMENT INCOME - Qualifying income is determined from the Tax Returns

specifications: Must be included as part of the complete tax return package
Must show gross receipts/income from 1099 sources
Must show business expenses for income calculation
Must show net profit or loss
Must identify the business name and principal business activity
Must support calculation of qualifying income with add-backs per guidelines (depreciation, business use of home, amortization/casualty loss)
reasons_needed: Schedule C is required for 1099 income borrowers filing as sole proprietors
Guidelines require Schedule C analysis to determine qualifying income from self-employment/1099 sources
Expense analysis and add-backs (depreciation, depletion, business use of home, amortization) are calculated from Schedule C
Net income from Schedule C is used for DTI qualification
document_requests.guideline_reference: QUALIFYING INCOME (S/E FULL DOC) - When analyzing Tax Returns: Depreciation, Depletion, Business use of home, Amortization/casualty loss may be added back

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 months of account activity
Must include all pages of each statement, including blank pages
Must show borrower name matching loan application
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or last 4 digits masked)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of the note date per NQMF guidelines
Must include all accounts intended for funds to close and reserves
Online statements acceptable if they include all required information
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close per NQMF guidelines
20% down payment ($82,200) must be sourced from verified accounts
Closing costs and prepaid items must be supported by liquid assets
Reserve requirements must be verified from acceptable asset sources
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower identity
Transaction history needed to identify any large deposits requiring additional sourcing
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS section: Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and applicable reserves

specifications: Must show copy of earnest money deposit check (front and back if cashed)
Must show bank statement reflecting the cleared EMD withdrawal
Must show receipt from escrow company or title company confirming deposit
EMD amount must match purchase contract terms
Deposit must be from borrower's verified account
If EMD was wired, must include wire confirmation showing source account
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be sourced from borrower's own verified funds
EMD amount is credited toward down payment and must be documented
Escrow/title company receipt confirms funds are being held for transaction
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS: Funds must be verified from acceptable sources for down payment

specifications: Must document source of all funds used for down payment, closing costs, and reserves
Must show clear paper trail from source to borrower's verified account
Any deposits greater than 50% of qualifying monthly income must be explained and sourced
Must include supporting documentation for any non-payroll deposits
Must confirm all funds are from acceptable sources per program guidelines
reasons_needed: 20% down payment ($82,200) requires verification of acceptable fund sources
NQMF guidelines require funds from acceptable sources for all transaction components
Large deposits identified in bank statements may require additional sourcing documentation
Ensures funds are not from prohibited sources or undisclosed loans
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS: Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources
document_requests.conditional: Required if large deposits are identified in bank statements

specifications: Must be a tri-merge credit report (RMCR) from Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax
Must include all borrowers on the loan with valid Social Security numbers
Must be dated within 120 days of note date
Must show credit scores from all three bureaus
Must include complete tradeline details with payment history
Must include verification of all credit references on the loan application
Must certify public record searches for each city of residence in last 2 years
Must show inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and alerts
Must display housing/mortgage payment history if available on credit
Must meet minimum credit score requirements per Flex Supreme program matrix
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate Flex Supreme program eligibility

Tradeline requirements must be verified: 3 tradelines reporting 12+ months with activity in last 12 months, OR 2 tradelines reporting 24+ months with activity in last 12 months, OR 24 month mortgage history with 0x30x12
Credit report is needed to identify any credit events (bankruptcy, foreclosure, short sale, modification) that would require seasoning verification
Housing history verification is required for all programs per NQMF guidelines
Public record search is required to identify any undisclosed judgments or liens
Inquiries must be reviewed to identify potential undisclosed debt obligations
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Underwriting Guidelines - Credit Report section

specifications: Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Form) for single-family residence
Must identify property type as single-family residence and unit count
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation at 80%
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date; if older, recertification required per NQMF guidelines
Must include all pages and addenda
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables used
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with gross living area calculations
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any areas with physical deterioration or renovations
Must include comparable sales analysis and appraiser certification
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, health/safety issues, or inspection requirements
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C1-C4; C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm property meets minimum property standards: year-round use, no environmental hazards, no health/safety violations
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must include Certification and Statement of Limiting Conditions signed by appraiser
Must include copy of appraiser's Errors and Omissions Insurance Policy valid as of appraisal date
Appraiser must be properly licensed or certified in California
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting per NQMF guidelines
LTV/CLTV eligibility at 80% depends on appraised value
Property type and condition affect program eligibility under Flex Supreme
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
California property must be appraised by California-licensed appraiser per Dodd-Frank requirements
Property must meet minimum standards: average or better condition, no health/safety violations, year-round residential use

specifications: Must include Submission Summary Report (SSR) from UCDP portal
SSR Score must be 2.5 or less to satisfy appraisal review requirements
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal
Must identify any warnings, messages, or flags requiring resolution
Must be submitted within timeframe required by program guidelines
reasons_needed: NQMF appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less satisfies review requirement per NQMF guidelines
Required for collateral risk assessment and value validation
document_requests.conditions_for_waiver: Alternative review products may satisfy requirement if SSR score exceeds 2.5: House Canary AVM (FSD max 0.10), Clear Capital Desk Review (within 10% tolerance), Field Review, or Second Full Appraisal

specifications: Must be provided by FEMA-certified flood determination company
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation (Section B4)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or Zone V
Must indicate whether NFIP map exists (Section B5)
Must include determination date
Section D must clearly indicate whether flood insurance is required
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require determination of whether subject property is in flood zone per FEMA
Flood insurance is required for any property in SFHA (Flood Zone A or V)
Appraisal report flood zone must be reconciled with flood certificate
California properties may be in various flood zones requiring verification
document_requests.conditions_for_waiver: None - flood determination is always required to establish flood zone status

specifications: Must name all borrowers (Veronica Marie Montes) as named insured matching note
Must identify subject property address agreeing with note/security instrument
Must include standard mortgagee clause (not loss payable clause)
Must provide coverage on replacement cost basis (not actual cash value)
Coverage must protect against fire and standard extended coverage perils: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must include policy number, loan number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective and expiration dates - policy must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must show premium amount, coverage amounts, and deductible
Must include notice of cancellation provision requiring written notice to mortgagee
Must be signed and dated by agent
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required per NQMF guidelines to protect collateral against loss or damage
Coverage must be on replacement cost basis - actual cash value policies not acceptable
All persons holding title must be named insured to ensure borrower has full rights to policy
Mortgagee clause required to protect lender interest in collateral
Policy must remain effective through and beyond closing to ensure continuous coverage
document_requests.conditions_for_waiver: None - property insurance is always required

specifications: Must identify borrower/purchaser name matching loan file (Veronica Marie Montes)
Must identify subject property address and legal description matching 1007 E Orange Grove Blvd, Pasadena CA 91104
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and Schedule B exceptions
Must show title insurer and effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of anticipated closing
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF guidelines
Title insurer must be licensed and qualified to do business in California
Must identify requirements to be satisfied prior to policy issuance
reasons_needed: Title must confirm clear ownership and insurable interest for the purchase transaction
NQMF guidelines require 24-month title history review for vesting inconsistencies or flipping activity
Lien position and exceptions must be reviewed to ensure first lien position without adverse exceptions
Title exceptions must be evaluated against NQMF allowable exceptions criteria
Required to verify no judgments, liens, or tax liens affecting the property per NQMF guidelines
document_requests.trigger_reason: Standard requirement for all purchase transactions - underwriter must review title exceptions and confirm lien position

specifications: Must identify current owner/seller of the subject property
Must include complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book, page, document number, recording date)
Must identify property address matching 1007 E Orange Grove Blvd, Pasadena CA 91104
Must show manner of vesting (e.g., fee simple, joint tenants, etc.)
Deed type must be identified (Grant Deed, Warranty Deed, etc.)
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require vesting history review for inconsistencies
Confirms seller has legal authority to convey the property
Legal description must align with title commitment and loan documents
Chain of title review required per 24-month title history guideline requirement
document_requests.trigger_reason: Standard requirement for purchase transactions - confirms property ownership and vesting for chain of title

STEP_00.summary: STEP_00 completed successfully. Parsed loan file for borrower Veronica Marie Montes with a $328,800 purchase loan at 80% LTV for a primary residence SFR in California. The eligibility engine identified Flex Supreme as the program with 1099 (12 month) income documentation. 9 documents were parsed from the manifest including Purchase Contract, URLA 1003, Bank Statement, 1099-NEC, and Paystub. Documents were routed to facets: 4 crosscutting, 2 income, 2 assets, 1 title_closing, 1 compliance. One missing core variable identified: DTI. No contradictions detected between XML and submitted documents.

STEP_01.summary: Cross-cutting document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction. Generated 4 essential cross-cutting document requests: (1) Government-Issued Photo ID for identity verification, (2) IRS Form 4506-C for tax transcript authorization as required by NQMF guidelines for 1099 income documentation, (3) Fully Executed Purchase Contract to verify the $411,000 purchase price and 80% LTV calculation, and (4) Occupancy Certification to confirm primary residence intent. No overlay conflicts detected. No contradictions requiring Letters of Explanation were identified in the loan file.

STEP_02.summary: Income document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes 1099 (12 month) income qualification under Flex Supreme program. Generated 4 income-specific document requests: (1) 1099 Forms showing 12 months of income from the same source, (2) Tax Return - 1040 with all schedules for income calculation, (3) Schedule C for self-employment/1099 income analysis including add-backs for depreciation and business expenses, and (4) Verification of Self-Employment to confirm business existence within 30 days of closing per NQMF guidelines. All documents are required to support income continuity, stability, and qualification calculations for the 1099 income documentation type.

STEP_03.summary: Asset document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction at 80% LTV. Based on NQMF guidelines requiring assets dated within 90 days of note date from acceptable sources, generated 3 asset-specific document requests: (1) Personal Bank Statements covering 2 months with complete transaction history to verify funds to close and reserves, (2) Earnest Money Deposit Verification including copy of EMD check and escrow receipt for purchase transaction, and (3) Source of Funds Documentation for the $82,200 down payment. No gift funds, business funds, investment accounts, or retirement accounts were indicated in the scenario, so those document types were appropriately skipped per the relevance gate. The documentation will support verification of 20% down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, and reserve requirements.

outputs.skip_reason: No indicators in scenario for gift funds, business funds, investment/retirement assets, or large deposit flags
outputs.guideline_applied: NQMF ASSETS - Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date and evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources

STEP_04.summary: Credit document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction under Flex Supreme program. Applied strict Relevance Gate rules to scenario: purchase transaction with no indicated credit events, no existing mortgages to verify, and no flags for disputes or fraud alerts. Per NQMF guidelines requiring tri-merge credit report for all borrowers, generated 1 credit document request: Credit Report with full specifications for 3-bureau merge, tradeline verification (3 tradelines 12+ months OR 2 tradelines 24+ months), credit score validation, housing history review, and public records certification. VOM, VOR, Bankruptcy/Foreclosure/Short Sale documentation, Credit Event LOE, Tradeline Supplement, Dispute Resolution, and Fraud Alert Verification were all SKIPPED per Relevance Gate as no triggering conditions exist in the scenario.

skip_reasons.Mortgage Payment History: No existing mortgages indicated - purchase transaction
skip_reasons.VOM: No existing mortgage payment gaps to verify
skip_reasons.VOR: Borrower purchasing primary residence - housing history via credit report

STEP_05.summary: Property and appraisal document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction of a primary residence SFR in California at 80% LTV under Flex Supreme program. Applied strict Relevance Gate rules to scenario facts: generated 4 property-specific document requests. (1) Appraisal Report on FNMA Form 1004 with full specifications for SFR including interior/exterior photos, comparable sales, property condition C1-C4, appraiser licensing, and E&O insurance. (2) SSR/UCDP Findings with score requirement of 2.5 or less per NQMF appraisal review process. (3) Flood Determination from FEMA-certified provider to establish flood zone status and determine if flood insurance required. (4) Property Insurance with replacement cost coverage, named insured matching borrowers, mortgagee clause, and extended coverage perils. Properly SKIPPED documents per Relevance Gate: Form 1007 Rent Schedule (primary residence, not investment), Condo Questionnaire (SFR, not condo), PUD Cert (SFR, not PUD), 1-4 Family Rider (primary residence, not investment), Appraisal Update/Recertification (future event), Field Review (future event), Property Inspection Report (no disaster indicated).

STEP_06.summary: Title and closing document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction of a primary residence SFR in California at 80% LTV under Flex Supreme program. Applied strict Relevance Gate rules to purchase scenario: generated 2 title-specific document requests. (1) Title Commitment required to review exceptions, confirm lien position, verify 24-month chain of title history, and ensure no judgments or liens affecting property per NQMF guidelines. (2) Vesting Deed to confirm seller ownership and verify chain of title. Payoff Statement and Mortgage Statement appropriately excluded as this is a purchase transaction with no existing lien to pay off. HOA documentation excluded as property is an SFR with no HOA indicated. Closing Disclosure and Settlement Statement excluded per Relevance Gate as these documents do not exist at underwriting time and are generated at closing.

documents_excluded.payoff_statement: Not applicable - purchase transaction (no existing lien)
documents_excluded.hoa_documentation: Not triggered - SFR with no HOA indicated
documents_excluded.closing_disclosure: Excluded per Relevance Gate - closing-stage document
documents_excluded.settlement_statement: Excluded per Relevance Gate - closing-stage document

STEP_07.summary: Compliance document needs analysis completed for Veronica Marie Montes primary residence purchase transaction under Flex Supreme program. Applied STRICT Relevance Gate rules to scenario facts: this is a clean loan with no compliance-specific triggers. No Business Purpose Affidavit needed (primary residence, not DSCR/investor program). No ITIN Documentation needed (borrower is not ITIN). No Entity/Trust Documentation needed (standard individual borrower structure). No U.S. Citizenship Documentation needed (no citizenship status concerns). No LOE for Declarations needed (no affirmative declarations flagged). Cross-cutting module already handles Government-Issued ID, 4506-C, and Occupancy Certification. Result: zero compliance-specific document requests generated, which is appropriate for a clean primary residence purchase loan.

analysis_summary.business_purpose_affidavit: NOT_TRIGGERED - Primary residence occupancy, not business purpose or DSCR/investor program
analysis_summary.itin_documentation: NOT_TRIGGERED - No ITIN borrower indicated in loan file
analysis_summary.entity_trust_documentation: NOT_TRIGGERED - Standard individual borrower, no entity or trust involved
analysis_summary.citizenship_documentation: NOT_TRIGGERED - No citizenship status concerns flagged
analysis_summary.loe_declarations: NOT_TRIGGERED - No affirmative declarations requiring explanation

outputs.relevance_gate_applied: STRICT - Compliance generates ONLY documents triggered by specific loan facts

STEP_08.summary: Document Request Merger and Ranker completed for Veronica Marie Montes purchase transaction under Flex Supreme program. Collected 17 document requests from Modules 01-07 (Cross-cutting: 4, Income: 3, Assets: 3, Credit: 1, Property: 4, Title: 2, Compliance: 0). After merging by document_type and context, consolidated to 15 unique document requests. Ranked by severity and priority: 2 P0 critical documents (1099 Forms and Tax Return 1040 for income eligibility), 12 P1 documents (including Government ID, 4506-C, bank statements, business license, CPA letter, appraisal report, preliminary title, title commitment, hazard insurance, and purchase contract), and 1 P2 document (large deposit verification if applicable). All 15 documents show status 'needed'. No overlay conflicts detected. Final output ready for delivery to underwriting.

Must support calculation of qualifying income with add-backs per guidelines (depreciation, business use of home, amortization/casualty loss)
reasons_needed: Tax returns are required to verify and calculate qualifying income for 1099 borrowers
Schedule C or E is needed to analyze self-employment/1099 income per NQMF guidelines
Tax return income must reconcile with 1099 forms to support income continuity and stability
Required documentation for income qualification under Flex Supreme program
Schedule C is required for 1099 income borrowers filing as sole proprietors
Guidelines require Schedule C analysis to determine qualifying income from self-employment/1099 sources
Expense analysis and add-backs (depreciation, depletion, business use of home, amortization) are calculated from Schedule C
Net income from Schedule C is used for DTI qualification
document_requests.guideline_reference: SELF-EMPLOYMENT INCOME - Qualifying income is determined from the Tax Returns

specifications: Must show copy of earnest money deposit check (front and back if cashed)
Must show bank statement reflecting the cleared EMD withdrawal
Must show receipt from escrow company or title company confirming deposit
EMD amount must match purchase contract terms
Deposit must be from borrower's verified account
If EMD was wired, must include wire confirmation showing source account
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be sourced from borrower's own verified funds
EMD amount is credited toward down payment and must be documented
Escrow/title company receipt confirms funds are being held for transaction
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS: Funds must be verified from acceptable sources for down payment

specifications: Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Form) for single-family residence
Must identify property type as single-family residence and unit count
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation at 80%
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date; if older, recertification required per NQMF guidelines
Must include all pages and addenda
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables used
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with gross living area calculations
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any areas with physical deterioration or renovations
Must include comparable sales analysis and appraiser certification
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, health/safety issues, or inspection requirements
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C1-C4; C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm property meets minimum property standards: year-round use, no environmental hazards, no health/safety violations
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must include Certification and Statement of Limiting Conditions signed by appraiser
Must include copy of appraiser's Errors and Omissions Insurance Policy valid as of appraisal date
Appraiser must be properly licensed or certified in California
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting per NQMF guidelines
LTV/CLTV eligibility at 80% depends on appraised value
Property type and condition affect program eligibility under Flex Supreme
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
California property must be appraised by California-licensed appraiser per Dodd-Frank requirements
Property must meet minimum standards: average or better condition, no health/safety violations, year-round residential use

specifications: Must include Submission Summary Report (SSR) from UCDP portal
SSR Score must be 2.5 or less to satisfy appraisal review requirements
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal
Must identify any warnings, messages, or flags requiring resolution
Must be submitted within timeframe required by program guidelines
reasons_needed: NQMF appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less satisfies review requirement per NQMF guidelines
Required for collateral risk assessment and value validation

specifications: Must be provided by FEMA-certified flood determination company
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation (Section B4)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or Zone V
Must indicate whether NFIP map exists (Section B5)
Must include determination date
Section D must clearly indicate whether flood insurance is required
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require determination of whether subject property is in flood zone per FEMA
Flood insurance is required for any property in SFHA (Flood Zone A or V)
Appraisal report flood zone must be reconciled with flood certificate
California properties may be in various flood zones requiring verification

specifications: Must name all borrowers (Veronica Marie Montes) as named insured matching note
Must identify subject property address agreeing with note/security instrument
Must include standard mortgagee clause (not loss payable clause)
Must provide coverage on replacement cost basis (not actual cash value)
Coverage must protect against fire and standard extended coverage perils: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must include policy number, loan number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective and expiration dates - policy must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must show premium amount, coverage amounts, and deductible
Must include notice of cancellation provision requiring written notice to mortgagee
Must be signed and dated by agent
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance is required per NQMF guidelines to protect collateral against loss or damage
Coverage must be on replacement cost basis - actual cash value policies not acceptable
All persons holding title must be named insured to ensure borrower has full rights to policy
Mortgagee clause required to protect lender interest in collateral
Policy must remain effective through and beyond closing to ensure continuous coverage

specifications: Must identify borrower/purchaser name matching loan file (Veronica Marie Montes)
Must identify subject property address and legal description matching 1007 E Orange Grove Blvd, Pasadena CA 91104
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and Schedule B exceptions
Must show title insurer and effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of anticipated closing
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF guidelines
Title insurer must be licensed and qualified to do business in California
Must identify requirements to be satisfied prior to policy issuance
reasons_needed: Title must confirm clear ownership and insurable interest for the purchase transaction
NQMF guidelines require 24-month title history review for vesting inconsistencies or flipping activity
Lien position and exceptions must be reviewed to ensure first lien position without adverse exceptions
Title exceptions must be evaluated against NQMF allowable exceptions criteria
Required to verify no judgments, liens, or tax liens affecting the property per NQMF guidelines

specifications: Must identify current owner/seller of the subject property
Must include complete legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book, page, document number, recording date)
Must identify property address matching 1007 E Orange Grove Blvd, Pasadena CA 91104
Must show manner of vesting (e.g., fee simple, joint tenants, etc.)
Deed type must be identified (Grant Deed, Warranty Deed, etc.)
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require vesting history review for inconsistencies
Confirms seller has legal authority to convey the property
Legal description must align with title commitment and loan documents
Chain of title review required per 24-month title history guideline requirement

specifications: Must include signed borrower certification of intent to occupy as primary residence
Must specify intended occupancy date (within 60 days of closing for primary residence)
Must acknowledge penalties for occupancy fraud
Must be consistent with loan application declarations

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy must be certified to confirm eligibility for 80% LTV and Flex Supreme program requirements
Occupancy type drives documentation requirements, reserve requirements, and pricing
